page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture, utilizing AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage. Network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average category, multi-core performance is highly competitive. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core execution, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations placing in the top tier. However, LLM inference tasks show mixed results, with prompt processing for a small 135M model ranking in the bottom tier, while processing for a medium 7B model reaches the top 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gn.48xlarge offers high resource density by combining a large memory footprint with 192 dedicated physical cores, reducing virtualization overhead. This profile is suited for memory-intensive and concurrent workloads. Favorable benchmark results in Passmark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput indicate suitability for large-scale database deployments, in-memory caching, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and ARMv9 arm64 architecture. It features 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities contrasted with top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth results. Specifically, it excels in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and memory read/write operations. Conversely, LLM inference speeds vary, with small model prompt processing performing in the lower quartile. Given its high memory density and strong multi-core throughput, this instance is suited for database operations, Redis caching, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.48xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 192 dedicated physical cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-density ARMv9 memory-optimized platform featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

1536.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and web serving
